item.title: Apple Raises Mac and iPad Prices to Counter Memory Shortages
item.description: Apple Inc. took the extreme measure of raising prices of Macs, iPads and home devices on Thursday, seeking to offset cost hikes caused by an unprecedented shortage of memory chips and storage.

item.title: US Inflation Climbs to Three-Year High, Spending Picks Up
item.description: US consumer spending accelerated in May even as prices rose at the fastest pace in more than three years, suggesting Americans are powering through the fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: Bank of England to Spend £300 Million After Bernanke Review
item.description: The Bank of England ramped up spending by 10% in 2025/26 as it laid off staff and boosted investment following a critical review by Ben Bernanke.

item.title: Europe’s Heat Wave Is a Power-Market Pressure Test
item.description: Both France and the UK have seen temperature records this week that drove up electricity prices and strained the grid.

item.title: Braskem Seeks Emergency Protection as Debt Talks Hit Snags
item.description: Brazilian petrochemical giant Braskem SA filed for emergency protection against creditors as talks for an out-of-court solution to its mounting debt woes hit snags.

item.title: Wall Street Sees AI Backlash as Rising Risk to Tech-Fueled Rally
item.description: Market professionals already on edge about the staying power of soaring artificial intelligence stocks are starting to grapple with another risk: public anger toward the technology.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Raises Economic Growth Forecast as Stimulus Flows
item.description: Brazil’s central bank raised its 2026 economic growth forecast as a strong labor market and government spending support activity while also undermining tight monetary policy that seeks to slow inflation to target.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 25, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures climb 0.7% as of 7:42 a.m. in New York. Stocks are set to rally as a forecast from Micron reinvigorates the AI trade.

item.title: US Customs Broadens AI Use in Push to Strengthen Enforcement
item.description: Complying with Trump’s tariffs is growing in complexity, requiring new tech to avoid penalties

item.title: BofA’s Raedler Says Defensives Ready to Roar if AI Trade Falters
item.description: Investors should switch their focus to defensive sectors if the artificial intelligence trade starts to unravel, according to Sebastian Raedler, head of European equity strategy at Bank of America Corp.

item.title: Samsung Teams Up With US Battery Startup Seeking Pentagon Cash
item.description: Samsung SDI Co. has agreed to provide $20 million and engineering support to Forge Nano Inc., a US battery startup aiming to build cells for the US defense and aerospace industries at a new plant in North Carolina.

item.title: AI Revenue Starts to Exceed a Key Infrastructure Cost But Not By Much
item.description: Google and Amazon will need to sustain premium pricing and strong user growth if they are to recoup their investments

item.title: Private Credit’s Big Arbitrage Trade Gains Backing From Advisers
item.description: On paper, it seems like a no-brainer trade: Cash out of one private credit fund at 100% of net asset value and plow the money back into a similar vehicle that’s trading at a substantially discounted price.

item.title: OpenAI, Anthropic and SpaceX Are Shaking Up VC Fundraising
item.description: The potential for big gains allows firms to raise record amounts of money

item.title: US Corporate Profits Hit Levels That Alarmed Harry Truman’s Aides
item.description: US company profits are booming, but in the past such bonanzas have triggered angst over the economy.

item.title: Iraq Walks Back Threat on Leaving OPEC Over Output Limits
item.description: Iraq isn’t considering leaving OPEC, the nation’s oil ministry said, just a few hours after saying that doing so was an eventual possibility.

item.title: A Veteran Apple Designer Reimagines the Golf Cart
item.description: Julian Hoenig’s Amble is dreaming up next-generation course cruisers — and aiming far beyond the fairway.

item.title: Fannie, Freddie Boost Risk to Levels That Once Shook Wall Street
item.description: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac are taking on more interest-rate risk in their rapidly growing investment portfolios, driving a key gauge of their exposure to levels that rattled Wall Street two decades ago.

item.title: US Customs Ramps Up AI Investment in Push to Sharpen Enforcement
item.description: US customs authorities, under pressure to improve enforcement of President Donald Trump’s trade war, are increasingly turning to artificial intelligence to catch tariff dodgers and spot illicit goods before cargo even reaches the country.

item.title: AI Sales Start to Justify Data-Center Spending Boom, Report Says
item.description: Revenue from artificial intelligence has reached a tipping point, showing that the hundreds of billions of dollars tech companies are spending on it may be economically sustainable, according to a report from research firm Exponential View.

item.title: Conservatives Urge Overturn of Trump’s Biofuel Blending Quotas
item.description: Nearly three dozen conservatives — including longtime allies of President Donald Trump — are pushing the House to overturn biofuel-blending quotas imposed by his administration, arguing they are stoking consumer costs before an election set to hinge on economic concerns.

item.title: ECB Rate-Hike Forecasts Pared Back as Oil Regains Pre-War Level
item.description: A sharp drop in oil prices amid progress in Middle-East peace talks has prompted some economists to scale back expectations for European Central Bank interest-rate increases.

item.title: Micron Delivers the AI Reassurance Wall Street Was Craving
item.description: The US memory-chip maker released a quarterly sales forecast that crushed estimates, easing AI fears.

item.title: Japan Unveils Multi-Year Framework as Part of Budget Reform
item.description: Japan will create a new multi-year framework to finance policies aimed at boosting its growth potential as part of Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s overhaul of the country’s budgeting process.

item.title: Gilt Returns Climb to Three-Month High After Oil-Fueled Recovery
item.description: Returns on UK government bonds are the highest in three months as tumbling oil prices offset investor concerns about unpredictable politics.

item.title: AI IPO Wealth Seen Bolstering Luxury Industry Hurt by Iran War
item.description: The luxury sector is set to grow more slowly than previously predicted because of the Iran war, but wealth creation from the SpaceX listing and future US share sales by OpenAI and Anthropic could cushion the blow for struggling brands selling pricey handbags and watches, according to Bain & Co.

item.title: Russia Used Cellebrite Technology in Case Against Activist, Report Says
item.description: Russian authorities used technology manufactured by Israeli digital forensics company Cellebrite to aid the prosecution of a prominent pro-democracy activist, according to a new report.

item.title: NLB Chief Touts 40% Premium, Heritage in Bidding War for Addiko
item.description: After two years of trying to get a foothold in neighboring Croatia by buying Addiko Bank AG, Slovenia’s largest lender isn’t ready to give up.

item.title: Switzerland’s Capital Plan for UBS Wins Wholehearted IMF Backing
item.description: The Swiss government’s plan to make UBS Group AG hold significantly more capital was praised wholeheartedly by the International Monetary Fund.

item.title: Microsoft, Amazon Cloud Units Face Tough EU Rules for Big Tech
item.description: Microsoft Corp.’s Azure and Amazon Web Services face tough European Union guardrails after regulators said they should be targeted by the bloc’s Digital Markets Act.
